title: The Great Un-monolithing
shortDesc: One giant app became many small, well-behaved services. Readers never noticed. That was the point.
body:
The old system was one enormous application that did everything and feared nothing, least of all my weekends. We took it apart piece by piece: a message queue in the middle, services around it, Kubernetes herding the containers.

The success metric was silence. No reader ever noticed a thing: the paper published every single day while we swapped the engine mid-flight.
moral: Moral: the best migrations are the boring ones.
postcardTo: everyone who reads the news without thinking about queues

title: Newsroom plumbing
shortDesc: A publishing workflow a real paper runs on, every day, deadlines and all.
body:
Reporters write, editors edit, and somewhere between 'done' and 'published' lives an entire hidden city of pipes. I designed and built a good chunk of that city: APIs, storage, caching, the works.

It runs on deadlines that do not move for anybody. The plumbing holds. Mostly nobody knows it exists, which is exactly how plumbing should work.
moral: Moral: if they never think about you, you did it right.

title: 100k good mornings
shortDesc: An automated newsletter that beats the sunrise into six figures of inboxes.
body:
Every morning, before coffee exists, an automated system assembles the day’s news and delivers it to over 100,000 inboxes. It has done this more reliably than I get out of bed.

The fun part was making it boring: retries, queues, monitoring, and a healthy paranoia about what happens at 4am when nobody is watching.
moral: Moral: automation is just kindness to your future self, scheduled.
postcardTo: 100,000 people who are not awake yet

title: Meo Wave Race
shortDesc: A cat/boat racing game for the CS50 final. The cat is the boat. Ask me anything.
body:
For the CS50 final I made a racing game where a cat is also a boat. I modeled the cat. I modeled the waves. I wrote shaders. I regret nothing.

It shipped, it runs, and it remains my entire game development career: one game made, one game finished, undefeated.
postcardTo: anyone who says scope creep is inevitable
postcardFrom: a cat that is also a boat

title: This website
shortDesc: The site you're on. Rebuilt whenever the mood strikes, which is often.
body:
You are here. Previous versions had a space theme and a particle system that heated homes across Pittsburgh. This one has a lighthouse, postcards, and a hobby graveyard.

It gets rebuilt whenever the mood strikes, which historically is often. Consider this postcard a snapshot of a moving target.
moral: Moral: the portfolio is also the hobby.
postcardTo: future justin, who will redesign this

title: The home lab
shortDesc: Plex, HTPC, and a small fleet of machines that mostly behave.
body:
A Plex server, an HTPC, CachyOS on the desktop, and a small fleet of machines that mostly behave. This is the one hobby that survives every purge, because it is just the day job wearing pajamas.

Current status, as always: one tweak from perfect. The tweak changes weekly. Perfect remains at a fixed distance, like the horizon.
postcardTo: the family, who just wanted to watch a movie
